title: feat: implement runDuplicateCheck in lib/duplicate.js
description:
Create lib/duplicate.js with runDuplicateCheck(filePaths) function. Runs jscpd via child_process using CONFIG values, reads the JSON report it writes to CONFIG.DUPLICATE_OUTPUT_DIR (a temp path), parses it, and returns a structured array.

RETURN SHAPE (mirrors jscpd JSON output directly):
  Array<{
    firstFile:  { name: string, start: number, end: number },
    secondFile: { name: string, start: number, end: number },
    lines:  number,
    tokens: number
  }>

JSCPD INVOCATION:
  jscpd --min-lines CONFIG.DUPLICATE_MIN_LINES --min-tokens CONFIG.DUPLICATE_MIN_TOKENS
        --ignore-pattern CONFIG.DUPLICATE_IGNORE_PATTERNS
        --reporters json --output CONFIG.DUPLICATE_OUTPUT_DIR
        [filePaths...]

MOCK STRATEGY: mock execSync (child_process) for the jscpd call; mock fs.readFileSync for reading the output JSON; mock fs.existsSync to control whether the output file exists.

BEHAVIORAL TEST LIST (one RED test before each GREEN):
1. 'runDuplicateCheck returns empty array when filePaths is empty without calling jscpd'
   Predicted failure: received undefined or thrown error
2. 'runDuplicateCheck returns empty array when jscpd output file does not exist'
   Predicted failure: received undefined or fs.readFileSync called unexpectedly
3. 'runDuplicateCheck parses jscpd JSON and returns firstFile/secondFile/lines/tokens for each duplicate'
   Predicted failure: received [] (not yet parsing)
4. 'runDuplicateCheck returns empty array when jscpd exits non-zero'
   Predicted failure: thrown error propagated

Definition of done: all 4 tests green, no stubs, exports runDuplicateCheck from lib/duplicate.js.
notes: TDD ordering: test 3 (happy path parse) before test 1 (empty input). Reason: stub return [] makes tests 1, 2, 4 vacuously green — test 3 is the first genuine RED.
close_reason: Closed

title: feat: integrate jscpd findings into pr-metrics.yml PR comment
description:
Require lib/duplicate.js in pr-metrics.yml. Run runDuplicateCheck against the already-computed prodFiles array (map to filenames). Add a Duplication section to the PR comment when findings exist; omit the section silently when findings array is empty.

PR COMMENT SECTION FORMAT (exact strings matter for CHECK):
  ## Duplicate Code
  Found N duplicate block(s) across the changed files:
  - `path/to/file.js:10-25` duplicates `path/to/other.js:5-20` (15 lines, 120 tokens)
  (one bullet per finding, capped at 10 — add 'and N more...' if truncated)

When no findings: section is omitted entirely (no 'No duplicates found' line).

VERIFICATION (no unit tests for YAML): grep audit confirms
  - require('./lib/duplicate') appears in the script block
  - runDuplicateCheck is called with prodFiles.map(f => f.filename)
  - '## Duplicate Code' header string is present
  - commentParts.filter(Boolean) already handles optional sections (no extra guard needed)

Definition of done: grep audit passes, PR comment renders section only when findings exist.
close_reason: Closed

title: feat: integrate semantic findings into pr-metrics.yml
description:
Extend pr-metrics.yml Duplication section to include Layer 2 semantic findings when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is present. Degrade gracefully when key is absent — consistent with local-code-metrics.js pattern.

LAYER INDICATOR STRINGS (exact, appended to Duplication section footer):
  When both layers ran:
    '_Layer 1 (static) and Layer 2 (semantic) ran._'
  When Layer 1 only (no API key):
    '_Layer 1 (static) ran. Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to enable semantic analysis._'

SEMANTIC FINDINGS FORMAT (appended after static findings list):
  ### Semantic Similarity
  - `file1.js` / `file2.js`: similarity description (confidence: high)

When semantic layer returns no findings: omit the Semantic Similarity subsection, still show layer indicator.

VERIFICATION: grep audit confirms both indicator strings are present in the YAML; check conditional on ANTHROPIC_API_KEY mirrors existing pattern in the script block.

Definition of done: grep audit passes, both indicator strings are present, Layer 2 block is guarded by API key check.
close_reason: Closed

title: feat: add semantic duplicate detection via lib/claude.js
description:
Add analyzeDuplicatesWithClaude(client, filePaths, staticFindings) to lib/claude.js (same file as existing analyzeCommitWithClaude — consistent module boundary, no new file).

RETURN SCHEMA:
  Array<{
    file1:      string,   // relative path
    file2:      string,   // relative path
    similarity: string,   // one-sentence description of what is similar
    concern:    string,   // why this matters (e.g. 'same error-handling pattern repeated')
    confidence: 'high' | 'medium' | 'low'
  }>

SYSTEM PROMPT INTENT: Given a set of changed files and their neighbors, identify semantically similar functions or logic blocks that suggest copy-paste or AI-generated duplication — even when variable names or formatting differ. Static analysis has already flagged token-level matches (passed as staticFindings); the agent focuses on structural/conceptual similarity that jscpd would miss.

BEHAVIORAL TEST LIST:
1. 'analyzeDuplicatesWithClaude returns empty array when client is null'
   Predicted failure: throws TypeError (null client not guarded)
2. 'analyzeDuplicatesWithClaude constructs prompt including file contents and staticFindings count'
   Predicted failure: prompt does not mention staticFindings (not yet wired)
3. 'analyzeDuplicatesWithClaude parses structured response into file1/file2/similarity/concern/confidence'
   Predicted failure: returns raw string instead of array
4. 'analyzeDuplicatesWithClaude returns empty array when API response is malformed'
   Predicted failure: throws JSON parse error

MOCK STRATEGY: mock Anthropic client.messages.create; return fixture JSON matching return schema.

Definition of done: all 4 tests green, function exported from lib/claude.js.
close_reason: Closed

title: refactor: add duplicate detection config keys to lib/config.js
description:
Add four keys to the CONFIG object in lib/config.js:
  DUPLICATE_MIN_LINES:      5     (number)
  DUPLICATE_MIN_TOKENS:     50    (number)
  DUPLICATE_IGNORE_PATTERNS: []   (array of glob strings — caller populates for their repo; TEST_FILE_PATTERNS provides the right starting reference but is not hard-coded here)
  DUPLICATE_SCAN_PATHS:     []    (array of strings — empty means scan all provided file paths)

No behavioral change to any existing function.

BEHAVIORAL TEST LIST (name + predicted failure):
1. 'CONFIG.DUPLICATE_MIN_LINES defaults to 5'
   Predicted failure: received undefined (key missing)
2. 'CONFIG.DUPLICATE_MIN_TOKENS defaults to 50'
   Predicted failure: received undefined (key missing)
3. 'CONFIG.DUPLICATE_IGNORE_PATTERNS is an array'
   Predicted failure: received undefined (key missing)
4. 'CONFIG.DUPLICATE_SCAN_PATHS is an array'
   Predicted failure: received undefined (key missing)

Definition of done: all 4 tests green; npm test passes with no threshold changes; no other file modified.
close_reason: Closed

title: feat: implement resolveModuleNeighbors in lib/duplicate.js
description:
Add resolveModuleNeighbors(changedFilePaths) to lib/duplicate.js. Parses require()/import statements in each changed file using regex, resolves relative paths to absolute using path.resolve(path.dirname(sourceFile), importPath), appends .js if no extension, returns the union of changed files plus their direct (not transitive) local imports.

EDGE CASE DECISIONS (made now, not during implementation):
- Non-JS files (e.g. .yml, .json, .md): include in the returned set as-is, skip import parsing
- Files that do not exist on disk (deleted in PR): skip with fs.existsSync check, do not throw
- External/node_modules imports (no leading './'): ignore, do not resolve

BEHAVIORAL TEST LIST:
1. 'resolveModuleNeighbors returns changed files plus resolved local imports'
   Predicted failure: returns only the input files (import parsing not yet implemented)
2. 'resolveModuleNeighbors skips import resolution for non-JS files'
   Predicted failure: throws or returns unexpected paths for .yml input
3. 'resolveModuleNeighbors skips files that do not exist on disk'
   Predicted failure: throws ENOENT or returns broken paths
4. 'resolveModuleNeighbors ignores external/node_modules imports'
   Predicted failure: includes node_modules paths or throws on resolution

MOCK STRATEGY: mock fs.readFileSync for file contents; mock fs.existsSync for on-disk check.

Definition of done: all 4 tests green, function exported from lib/duplicate.js alongside runDuplicateCheck.
close_reason: Closed

title: Feature: Two-layer duplicate code detection in PR check (#42)
description: Add jscpd static detection (Layer 1, always-on) and agent-based semantic detection (Layer 2, conditional on ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) to pr-metrics.yml. All thresholds and ignore patterns live in lib/config.js as documented configurable parameters so the toolkit works across repos with different test conventions and directory structures.
notes:
Analysis: lib/config.js is single source of truth for all thresholds. lib/duplicate.js follows PR #33 pattern (require() in workflow). jscpd v5 uses --ignore-pattern flag, JSON output via --reporters json. Baseline: 0 duplicates in this repo production code at defaults. Other repos need to override DUPLICATE_IGNORE_PATTERNS. Layer 2 conditional on ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. Deliver Layer 1 first.
## ACT Retrospective — Two-Layer Duplicate Detection

**Key insight:** Task creation and task specification were collapsed into one pass during PLAN. The design questions (test lists, edge-case decisions, return schemas) were deferred until a mid-PLAN review question surfaced them. Without that question, DO would have started with underspecified tasks.

**Root cause:** The plan-beads-addon.md guidance described creating tasks but did not require behavioral acceptance criteria at creation time.

**Committed change:** Added 'Create Implementation Tasks with Behavioral Acceptance Criteria' section to plan-beads-addon.md in both installed skill and source repo. Each task now requires --acceptance (behavioral test list + edge-case decisions + definition of done) and --validate at creation time.

**Threshold calibration note:** DUPLICATE_MIN_LINES=5/DUPLICATE_MIN_TOKENS=50 correct for JS. Java/Python/Go need higher defaults per CLAUDE.md tuning guide.

**Design trade-off flagged:** showDuplicationSection evaluates true whenever anthropicClient is not null, meaning the Duplication section renders for clean PRs when API key is set. Deliberate — confirms the layer ran — but creates per-PR noise. Revisit if teams find it distracting.

title: Update churn distribution in local-code-metrics.js
description: TDD: update collectLocalMetrics.test.js first to expect churn_intensity_median and churn_intensity_p90 instead of additions_ratio_median and additions_ratio_p90, then implement. Replace local-code-metrics.js:181 ratio array with: metrics.map(m => Math.min(m.total_additions, m.total_deletions) / Math.max(m.total_additions + m.total_deletions, 1)). Rename summary fields from additions_ratio_median/additions_ratio_p90 to churn_intensity_median/churn_intensity_p90.
close_reason: Closed

title: Replace change_ratio with churn_intensity in lib/git.js
description: TDD: write failing tests first, then implement. Replace the string field change_ratio (which emits 'inf' for zero-deletion commits) with a numeric churn_intensity field using the formula: min(additions, deletions) / max(additions + deletions, 1). This is bounded [0.0, 0.5] and is always a number. Zero means pure addition or pure deletion; 0.5 means equal adds and deletes (maximum rework). Update __tests__/analyzeCommit.test.js: replace the two tests that assert change_ratio='inf' and change_ratio='2.00' with tests for churn_intensity. Add cases: no-deletions yields 0.0; equal adds/deletes yields 0.5; typical 10 adds / 5 deletes yields 0.333.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix unbounded churn ratio for statistically valid p50/p90
description: The additions/deletions ratio (additions / max(deletions, 1)) is unbounded. A commit of entirely new files yields ratio = additions/1, making p50/p90 meaningless (observed p90=446x). Replace with churn_intensity = min(additions, deletions) / max(additions + deletions, 1), which is bounded [0, 0.5] and measures rework fraction directly. This is more important than backwards compatibility with existing field names.
close_reason: Closed

Resolves GitHub issue #25.

Replace single-regex test file detection with isTestFile(filename). Replace computeStats calls with computeStatistics (takes sizes + timestamps arrays). Add velocity_commits_per_day to summary output — currently missing because inline computeVelocityTrend dropped it (computeVelocity returns {commits_per_day, trend}). The script block should have no helper function definitions after this step.

title: Step 1: Move isTestFile from lib/git.js to lib/metrics.js
description: isTestFile() in lib/git.js is a pure function — it only applies CONFIG.TEST_FILE_PATTERNS with no I/O or child_process dependency. It belongs in lib/metrics.js (alongside other classification logic) so workflows can require('./lib/metrics') cleanly without importing child_process-dependent functions. Steps: (1) copy isTestFile to lib/metrics.js and add to module.exports, (2) in lib/git.js import it from lib/metrics.js and re-export for existing callers, (3) run npm test — all 117 tests must pass unchanged.
close_reason: isTestFile moved to lib/metrics.js, re-exported from lib/git.js. 119/119 tests pass.

title: Eliminate workflow logic duplication via direct require() of lib/ modules
description: Epic: Replace inline JavaScript helper functions in .github/workflows/code-metrics.yml and pr-metrics.yml with require() calls to lib/config.js, lib/statistics.js, and lib/metrics.js. Currently all three components (local script + 2 workflows) duplicate threshold values and computation logic. The additions_ratio fix just required manual updates to 3 locations and nearly missed a divergent bug in pr-metrics.yml:90. Option A: actions/github-script@v7 runs in Node.js with require() resolving from GITHUB_WORKSPACE root after checkout — no build step or template engine needed. Shareable: lib/config.js, lib/statistics.js, lib/metrics.js. Not shareable: lib/git.js (child_process), lib/claude.js (local SDK auth).
close_reason: Closed

Note: GitHub workflow files (.github/workflows/) have additions ratio logic hard-coded in jq — these are out of scope for this cycle but should be tracked as follow-on work.

(2) Field names: additions_ratio_median -> net_additions_ratio_median, additions_ratio_p90 -> net_additions_ratio_p90. (3) Thresholds table: < 0.33 healthy (was < 2.0), 0.33-0.50 monitor (was 2.0-3.0), > 0.50 warning (was > 3.0). (4) Interpretation: '0.50 = more than half of all lines changed are net-new code'. (5) Remove the false positive note about net-new-file commits since the formula fix eliminates this issue.

title: Step 2: Replace ratio formula in local-code-metrics.js
description: Change line 181 from: const ratios = metrics.map(m => m.total_additions / (m.total_deletions || 1)); To: const ratios = metrics.map(m => (m.total_additions - m.total_deletions) / ((m.total_additions + m.total_deletions) || 1)); New formula is bounded [-1, +1]. Value 1.0 = all net-new code. Value 0.0 = balanced. Value -0.33 = cleanup. Human review gate after this step: Step 1 failing test must now pass.
close_reason: Formula replaced: bounded (additions-deletions)/(additions+deletions). Zero-total guard added.

title: Step 1: Write failing test documenting additions ratio bug
description: TDD red phase. In __tests__/statisticalDistribution.test.js (or new additionsRatio.test.js), write a test that passes an array of all-zero-deletion commits through the ratio computation and asserts median=1.0 (or close to it), not a large number like 500. This test must FAIL before the fix is applied. It defines the bug and the fix target. Files: __tests__/statisticalDistribution.test.js, local-code-metrics.js:181
close_reason: Failing test written and confirmed red: net_additions_ratio_median is undefined (field doesn't exist yet)

title: Fix additions ratio metric distortion from net-new-file commits
description: Epic: The additions ratio metric uses additions / max(deletions, 1), which yields values approaching infinity for commits with zero deletions (new files). This distorts both median and p90 of the distribution. The RETROSPECTIVE documented p90=446x as almost certainly dominated by net-new-file commits. Fix by replacing the formula with (additions - deletions) / (additions + deletions), which is bounded [-1, +1] and immune to the distortion. Rename summary fields from additions_ratio_* to net_additions_ratio_* to signal the semantic change. Update thresholds: old 3.0 warning maps to new 0.50.
close_reason: All 7 implementation steps complete. Formula fixed, fields renamed, spec updated, tests green.

Include example override values for common repo conventions (Java src/test/, Python tests/, Go _test.go). Note that defaults were validated against this repo baseline (0 production duplicates).

title: CHECK: Verify duplicate detection against acceptance criteria

close_reason: Closed

title: ACT: Retrospective for duplicate detection PDCA cycle
description:
Facilitate retrospective on the duplicate detection implementation cycle using the /act skill. Store insights to the beads epic.

RETROSPECTIVE FOCUS AREAS:
1. Threshold calibration — DUPLICATE_MIN_LINES=5/DUPLICATE_MIN_TOKENS=50 produced 8 false positives on this repo at defaults; were the thresholds right for other repos (Java/Python codebases with more boilerplate)? Should defaults be higher?
2. jscpd temp file strategy — jscpd writes to DUPLICATE_OUTPUT_DIR; is /tmp adequate or should this be a genuinely configurable path for CI environments with restricted tmp access?
3. Layer 2 scope boundary — resolveModuleNeighbors uses direct imports only (not transitive). Was that the right call for semantic detection, or does it miss too much context?
4. Config key documentation quality — are the four config keys (MIN_LINES, MIN_TOKENS, IGNORE_PATTERNS, SCAN_PATHS) named and described clearly enough for teams using this on unfamiliar repos?
5. TDD discipline — note any RED tests that failed differently than predicted and what that indicated.

Outputs: retrospective stored on the epic via 'bd update <epic-id> --notes'; any config adjustments captured as follow-on issues if warranted.

Definition of done: /act skill facilitated, insights stored to epic, any new issues created for follow-on work.

title: Fix: Replace unbounded additions/deletions churn ratio with bounded addition fraction
notes:
## Retrospective

Session goal: Replace unbounded additions/max(deletions,1) churn ratio with bounded addition fraction [0,1], fixing p50/p90 distortion from net-new-file commits.

What worked:
- Choosing additions/(additions+deletions) over Winsorization/log-transform — naturally bounded, no arbitrary cap, semantically precise. Decision locked in PLAN, no mid-cycle drift.
- computeStatistics needed zero changes — confirmed clean architectural separation between stat function and input construction.
- @ts-check typedef caught change_ratio type mismatch immediately when implementation changed from string to number|null.
- Naming the metric semantically (addition_fraction not ratio_v2) made rename obvious and test assertions self-documenting.

What to improve:
- Step 3 RED test passed immediately because it tested computeStatistics with already-bounded inputs, not the actual integration. Locate tests at the integration boundary (summary JSON output) when the bug is in the caller, not the callee.
- Step 3+4 collapsed into one unit in practice — plan should merge them when test and implementation always land in the same commit.
- change_ratio computed in lib/git.js, null-filtering inline in local-code-metrics.js — minor asymmetry to watch if ratios logic grows.

Key insights:
- Write the integration-level failing test first (what does summary JSON look like?), then unit tests. Work inward from observable output.
- 'Backwards compatibility explicitly deprioritized' stated in PLAN freed clean field rename without aliases.
- When a RED test passes immediately, the bug is one layer up — rewrite the test at that layer.

Action items for next cycle:
1. Start: State which layer a RED test targets before writing it. If it passes immediately, move test up one layer.
2. Stop: Separate beads tasks for RED+GREEN pairs that always collapse into one commit.
3. Keep: Lock metric naming semantics in PLAN phase before implementation begins.
close_reason: ✓ Complete. Bounded addition fraction [0,1] replaces unbounded ratio. p90 no longer distorted by net-new-file commits.

title: Feature: Fix unbounded additions/deletions churn ratio
notes:
## Retrospective

Session goal: Replace unbounded additions/max(deletions,1) churn ratio with addition_proportion bounded [0,1].

What worked:
- Calling shots before each phase kept steps tight and stale-assertion failures expected, not surprising
- PLAN analysis ruled out capped ratio and log-diff before coding — no mid-stream rethinking
- Proportion formula required zero config changes; statistically clean with no magic numbers

What to improve:
- After any field rename, grep the full test suite for the old name before marking step done
- REFACTOR scope check was too narrow: only updated the file being actively edited, missed collectLocalMetrics.test.js

Key insights:
- add grep -r <old_symbol> __tests__/ as explicit first action in any REFACTOR step involving renames
- Full suite run in CHECK is non-negotiable — caught the missed assertion that targeted-file run would have missed
- proportion = additions/(additions+deletions) is the right formula for any bounded churn metric; reuse in workflows

Action items for next cycle:
1. Add interface-grep step to REFACTOR: grep -r <old_symbol> __tests__/ before touching assertions
2. Smoke test (node local-code-metrics.js) belongs in CHECK definition-of-done for script-level changes
3. Keep shot-calling discipline — it was the single biggest accelerator this session
close_reason: All steps complete. change_ratio now bounded [0,1] proportion. p90 no longer inflated by net-new-file commits.

Feature: Add net_additions_ratio metric to fix unbounded churn ratio distortion
notes:
## Retrospective

Session goal: Fix unbounded additions/deletions churn ratio distorting p50/p90 by adding bounded net_additions_ratio alongside existing metric.

What worked:
- Add-not-replace approach: preserves backward compatibility, avoids silencing drift signals
- net_additions_ratio reuses totalLines already in scope — zero extra computation
- Full test list planned before opening files — DO phase had no surprises
- Called shots matched actual failures in both RED cycles

What to improve:
- Verify beads flag syntax (bd help update) at session start, not mid-PLAN
- mkl.2 (git.js field) and mkl.5 (typedef) were one atomic unit — should be one task

Key insights:
- For bounded ratio metrics: (add - del) / (add + del) is the canonical form; immune to new-file distortion
- When IDE surfaces a type error mid-step, resolve it immediately rather than deferring — keeps implementation coherent
- Called shot mismatches are the signal, not the noise — stop and diagnose when prediction != actual failure

Action items for next cycle:
1. Run bd help update once during PLAN to confirm flag names
2. Treat field addition + typedef update as one task when they are inseparable
3. Keep called shots — two for two this session

title: Implement: Claude API diff-level integration (optional)
description:
DO phase. Depends on: Design issue P2.
Files: local-code-metrics.js, package.json

Changes:
1. npm install @anthropic-ai/sdk
2. At startup: check process.env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY; if absent log "Claude analysis skipped (no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)" and proceed
3. Pre-filter: sort commits by (large_commit && additions > deletions*2) desc; take top AI_ANALYSIS_MAX_COMMITS (default 5)
4. For each filtered commit: run git show --stat {sha} + git diff {sha}^ {sha} -- (truncate combined output at 4000 chars)
5. Send to claude-sonnet-4-6 with structured output prompt asking for: { ai_confidence: 0-100, risk_score: 0-100, patterns: string[], architectural_concerns: string[], summary: string }
6. Annotate matching entries in commit metrics array with Claude findings
7. Write local_claude_analysis.json: { analyzed_at, model, commits_analyzed: N, results: [...] }
8. Add Claude findings to console report section
Node 18+ required for @anthropic-ai/sdk.
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement: DORA-aligned metrics in local-code-metrics.js
description:
DO phase. Depends on: Design issues (P1, P2).
Files: local-code-metrics.js

Changes:
1. Commit velocity metric: calculate commits/day over the analysis window; split into first-half / second-half to compute velocity trend (accelerating / stable / decelerating)
2. Additions-to-deletions ratio distribution: track ratio per commit, compute median and p90 across all commits (supplement existing per-commit large_commit flag)
3. Commit message quality scoring: regex for conventional commit prefixes (feat|fix|refactor|test|chore|docs|perf|ci|build|revert) + word count >= MESSAGE_QUALITY_MIN_WORDS; report % compliant
4. DORA archetype classification: map metric combinations to archetypes (harmonious-high-achiever / foundational-challenges / legacy-bottleneck / other) based on composite score across all 8 metrics
5. New CONFIG keys: MESSAGE_QUALITY_MIN_WORDS (default 10), VELOCITY_TREND_WINDOW
6. Add archetype + velocity trend + message quality to summary JSON and console report
close_reason: D1 complete. Added: computeVelocity(), scoreMessageQuality(), classifyDoraArchetype(). All wired into collectLocalMetrics() summary. 4 new CONFIG keys (MESSAGE_QUALITY_MIN_WORDS, AI_ANALYSIS_MAX_COMMITS, AI_DIFF_MAX_CHARS, AI_RISK_ADDITIONS_RATIO). 89/89 tests, typecheck clean, zero new deps.

title: Design: Claude API integration architecture
description:
PLAN phase — prerequisite to D3 implementation.

Architecture decisions:
- Diff extraction: git show --stat + git diff {sha}^ {sha} truncated at 4000 chars per commit
- Risk pre-filter: only analyze commits where large_commit=true AND additions > deletions * 2 (reduces API calls to ~3-5 per run)
- Graceful degradation: if no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in env, skip silently, add note to summary JSON
- New output file: local_claude_analysis.json with per-commit risk scores and findings
- Model: claude-sonnet-4-6 with structured JSON output (ai_confidence 0-100, risk_score 0-100, patterns[], architectural_concerns[])
- Prompt design: system prompt describing AI code patterns to detect (generic names, boilerplate CRUD, identical-structure repetition, absent domain language, boundary violations)
- GitHub workflows: same pattern, keyed by ANTHROPIC_API_KEY repository secret
close_reason: P2 complete. Architecture decisions: single-file approach (local-code-metrics.js only), conditional require() for graceful Node 16 degradation, sequential API calls (max 5), CLAUDE_SYSTEM_PROMPT as exported const, selectClaudeCommits pre-filter reuses AI_RISK_ADDITIONS_RATIO. Test mock pattern documented. New exports: computeStatistics, computeVelocity, scoreMessageQuality, classifyDoraArchetype, getAnthropicClient, selectClaudeCommits, analyzeWithClaude, getCommitDiff, CLAUDE_SYSTEM_PROMPT.

title: Design: DORA metric gap analysis and library selection
description:
PLAN phase — prerequisite to all implementation work.

Decisions to document:
- Map new metrics to DORA capabilities: velocity trend → DORA cap #5 (small batches), additions ratio → cap #4 (version control), message quality → cap #1 (clear AI stance)
- Confirm dependency additions: simple-statistics (stats), @anthropic-ai/sdk (Claude API), compromise (optional NLP)
- Define new CONFIG keys: AI_ANALYSIS_MAX_COMMITS (default 5), MESSAGE_QUALITY_MIN_WORDS (default 10), VELOCITY_TREND_WINDOW
- Confirm Node 18+ requirement (needed for @anthropic-ai/sdk; current requirement is ≥14)
- Decision: use compromise for message classification or pure regex? Regex is zero-dep, compromise is more accurate.
close_reason: P1 complete. Library decisions: simple-statistics YES, @anthropic-ai/sdk YES, compromise NO (regex sufficient), simple-git NO. Node 18+ required. 4 new CONFIG keys. All 8 metric definitions finalized. DORA coverage: capabilities 4 and 5 fully covered; 1/2/3/6/7 are acknowledged gaps. Findings documented in metrics-specification.md.

title: Epic: Add test infrastructure for local-code-metrics.js
description: Set up Jest test infrastructure with ESLint flat config and JSDoc type checking. Pure functions (parseGitLog, isTestFile, generateInsights) are tested directly; impure functions (runGitCommand, analyzeCommit, collectLocalMetrics) require child_process and fs mocks. Plan phase complete — see conversation for full analysis and file structure decisions.
notes:
## Retrospective

**Session goal:** Add Jest test infrastructure, ESLint flat config, JSDoc type checking, 63 tests for local-code-metrics.js

**What worked:**
- Pure-function-first test ordering (parseGitLog → isTestFile → generateInsights before mocked tests)
- mockExecSequence helper pattern for sequential execSync mocking
- Beads auto-close on child completion gave clean confirmation signal
- TDD caught generateInsights expectation mismatch immediately (3 insights not 2)

**What to improve:**
- PLAN underestimated @ts-check remediation: listed as 1-line change, was actually 8 edits + typedef block — scope annotation work explicitly in future PLAN phases
- Lint not run until all 63 tests written — missing globals.node in ESLint flat config produced 103 errors at the end; should run lint after each step

**Key insights:**
- ESLint v9 flat config requires explicit globals.node — @eslint/js recommended does not assume Node.js runtime
- Introducing @ts-check to untyped code is a full step, not a one-liner; cost = N functions * annotation effort
- Defensive catch blocks wrapping runGitCommand are unreachable because runGitCommand absorbs all git errors — document rather than force coverage

**Action items for next cycle:**
1. Add lint as a per-step gate in DO checklist, not just a final check
2. In PLAN, when introducing type system to untyped JS, list annotation work as explicit step with scope estimate
3. Keep pure-function-first test ordering — it is always the right call
close_reason: all steps complete
